name: HatchPatternTable
summary: All of the hatch pattern definitions contained in a rhino document.

methods.name: Add
methods.summary: Adds a new hatch pattern with specified definition to the table.

params.name: pattern
params.doc: definition of new hatch pattern. The information in pattern is copied. If patern.Name is empty the a unique name of the form "HatchPattern 01" will be automatically created.

returns.doc: >=0 index of new hatch pattern -1 not added because a hatch pattern with that name already exists or some other problem occurred.

methods.name: Delete(Int32)
methods.summary: Deletes a hatch pattern from the table.

params.name: hatchPatternIndex
params.doc: The index of the hatch pattern to delete.

methods.name: Delete(HatchPattern)
methods.summary: Deletes a hatch pattern from the table.

methods.name: Delete(Int32, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Delete(int hatchPatternIndex, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Deletes a hatch pattern from the table.

params.name: hatchPatternIndex
params.doc: The index of the hatch pattern to delete.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, no warning message box appears if hatch pattern cannot be deleted.

methods.name: Delete(HatchPattern, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Delete(HatchPattern item, bool quiet)
methods.summary: Deletes a hatch pattern from the table.

params.name: quiet
params.doc: If true, no warning message box appears if hatch pattern cannot be deleted.

methods.name: Find
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("ignoreDeleted is now ignored. Items are removed permanently now. Use FindName.")] public int Find(string name, bool ignoreDeleted)
methods.summary: Finds the hatch pattern with a given name. Search ignores case.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the hatch patter to be found.

params.name: ignoreDeleted
params.doc: true means don't search deleted hatch patterns.

returns.doc: Index of the hatch pattern with the given name. -1 if no hatch pattern found.

methods.name: FindId
methods.summary: Uses the guid to find a model component. Deleted objects cannot be found by id. The guid is the value that is stored in the .Id property. In a single document, no two active objects have the same guid. If an object is replaced with a new object, then the guid persists. For example, if the _Move command moves an object, then the moved object inherits its guid from the starting object. If the Copy command copies an object, then the copy gets a new guid. This guid persists through file saving/opening operations. This function will not find grip objects.

params.name: id
params.doc: ID of model component to search for.

returns.doc: Reference to the rhino object with the objectId or null if no such object could be found.

methods.name: FindIndex
methods.summary: Retrieves a HatchPattern object based on Index. This search type of search is discouraged. We are moving towards using only IDs for all tables.

returns.doc: A HatchPattern object, or null if none was found.

methods.name: FindName
methods.summary: Finds the hatch pattern with a given name. Search ignores case.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of the hatch patter to be found.

returns.doc: Hatch pattern with the given name. Null if no hatch pattern found.

methods.name: FindNameHash
methods.summary: Finds a HatchPattern given its name hash.

params.name: nameHash
params.doc: The name hash of the HatchPattern to be searched.

returns.doc: An Linetype, or null on error.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: Returns the enumerator that yields all items.

methods.name: Rename(Int32, String)
methods.signature: public bool Rename(int hatchPatternIndex, string hatchPatternName)
methods.summary: Renames a hatch pattern in the table.

params.name: hatchPatternIndex
params.doc: The index of the hatch pattern to rename.

methods.name: Rename(HatchPattern, String)
methods.signature: public bool Rename(HatchPattern item, string hatchPatternName)
methods.summary: Renames a hatch pattern in the table.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Returns the count of all items, including deleted ones.

properties.name: CurrentHatchPatternIndex
properties.summary: At all times, there is a "current" hatch pattern. Unless otherwise specified, new objects are assigned to the current hatch pattern. The current hatch pattern is never locked, hidden, or deleted.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Conceptually, the hatch pattern table is an array of hatch patterns. The operator[] can be used to get individual hatch patterns. A hatch pattern is either active or deleted and this state is reported by HatchPattern.IsDeleted.
